body_txt: Bob: Hey, Alice , have you ever noticed how sometimes a character will talk to another character about something that sounds like it's really about the show they're in, but it makes perfect sense in context? Alice: Yeah! Usually it sounds strained because it's hard to make this kind of dialogue sound completely natural. Especially with all of the Double Meaning Gags and Double Entendres . Bob: But if they can pull it off, it's usually good for a bit of comedy. Alice: This might be related to Lampshade Hanging , This Is the Part Where... , Conversational Troping or any other trope with Fourth Wall in its name. Bob: You mean like Fourth Wall Psych ? What about Aside Glance ? The inverse would be This Is Reality . Common subtropes are Diegetic Soundtrack Usage and Who Would Want to Watch Us? ... wait, why are we talking like this ? Charlie: See also "Clichéd Villain" Accusation . noreallife
